comment_text.sn26.10:1.1: This discourse is the same as SN 22.30.

root_text.sn26.10:1.2: “Yo kho, bhikkhave, rūpassa uppādo ṭhiti abhinibbatti pātubhāvo, dukkhasseso uppādo, rogānaṁ ṭhiti, jarāmaraṇassa pātubhāvo.
root_text.sn26.10:1.6: yo viññāṇassa uppādo ṭhiti abhinibbatti pātubhāvo, dukkhasseso uppādo, rogānaṁ ṭhiti, jarāmaraṇassa pātubhāvo.
root_text.sn26.10:1.7: Yo ca kho, bhikkhave, rūpassa nirodho vūpasamo atthaṅgamo, dukkhasseso nirodho, rogānaṁ vūpasamo, jarāmaraṇassa atthaṅgamo.
root_text.sn26.10:1.11: yo viññāṇassa nirodho vūpasamo atthaṅgamo, dukkhasseso nirodho, rogānaṁ vūpasamo, jarāmaraṇassa atthaṅgamo”ti.

translation_text.sn26.10:1.2: “Mendicants, the arising, continuation, and manifestation and regeneration of form is the arising of suffering, the continuation of diseases, and the manifestation of old age and death.
translation_text.sn26.10:1.3: The arising, continuation, and manifestation and regeneration of feeling,
translation_text.sn26.10:1.6: and consciousness is the arising of suffering, the continuation of diseases, and the manifestation of old age and death.
translation_text.sn26.10:1.7: The cessation, settling, and disappearance of form is the cessation of suffering, the settling of diseases, and the disappearance of old age and death.
translation_text.sn26.10:1.8: The cessation, settling, and ending of feeling,
translation_text.sn26.10:1.11: and consciousness is the cessation of suffering, the settling of diseases, and the disappearance of old age and death.”
translation_text.sn26.10:2.5: The Linked Discourses on arising are complete.
